jobs.basic_qualifications: - 3+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
jobs.description:
Do you want to be a leader in the team that takes last mile delivery in the Transportation models to the next generation? Do you have a solid analytical thinking and want to solve problems with solutions that will meet the growing worldwide need? If you have passion to learn and be curious, and want apply your programming and analytical skills to help Amazon grow, then Last Mile Delivery Service Partner (Last Mile DSP) is the team for you. We are looking for top notch SDEs to be part of our world class business software development team.
DSP (Delivery Service Partners) are medium and large sized 3rd party businesses delivering packages from amazon last mile delivery stations to the customer on behalf of Amazon. The team’s global mission is to become Amazon’s logistics labor capacity of choice by building the provider network required to support Amazon’s growth and meet customer expectations for any deliveries, at any time of the year through scalable, innovative, and low cost operations, and in a way that both delights customers and empowers DSPs and their Delivery Associates.
We are building world class technology that powers the DSPs. The teams own suite of systems/applications/tools/services to support the DSP program through its entire life cycle from marketing-vetting-on boarding through every day operations like scheduling, driver management, fleet management, compliance enforcement, payments and value added services. We aim for the DSP program to be the backbone of Amazon's transportation arsenal that is capable of handling all delivery types with scalable, innovative, and low-cost operations – all while working backward from DSP and delivery associate (DA) satisfaction.
The ideal candidate is a motivated self-starter that can work independently in a fast paced, ambiguous environment with limited supervision. The candidate should have strong communication skills and ability to priorities effectively to ensure timelines are met. Lastly, the candidate should have an ability to work effectively with cross-functional and global teams.

Key job responsibilities
As an SDE in the team, you will be responsible for -
• Ownership of product/feature end-to-end for all phases from the development to the production.
• Ensuring the developed features are scalable and highly available with no quality concerns.
• Work closely with peer engineers for refining the design and implementation.
• Create and execute appropriate quality plans, project plans, test strategies and processes for development activities in collaboration with business and project management efforts.
• Mentor and coach team SDEs

jobs.description:
We are embarking on a multi-year journey to improve the shopping experience for customers globally. Amazon Search team creates customer-focused search solutions and technologies that makes shopping delightful and effortless for our customers. Our goal is to understand what customers are looking for in whatever language happens to be their choice at the moment and help them find what they need in Amazon's vast catalog of billions of products.

As Amazon expands to new geographies and new interfaces, we are faced with the unique challenge of maintaining the bar on Search Quality due to the diversity in user preferences, multilingual search and data scarcity.

We are looking for an applied researcher to work on improving search on Amazon using NLP, ML, and DL technology. As an Applied Scientist, you will lead our efforts in query understanding, semantic matching, and ranking. As part of this role, you will develop high precision, high recall, and low latency solutions for search. Your solutions should work for all languages that Amazon supports and will be used in all Amazon locales world-wide. You will develop scalable science and engineering solutions that work successfully in production. You will work with leaders to develop a strategic vision and long term plans to improve search globally.

We are growing our collaborative group of engineers and applied scientists by expanding into new areas.

jobs.description:
Are you interested in helping Amazon make history and redefine the meaning of ‘fast’ in eCommerce? We are the Same Day Delivery Experience team and are in the early innings of reinventing the Amazon shopping experience to make Amazon the first place customers think to shop when they need something today, wherever they are in the world. We’re looking for a Sr. Software Development Engineer for the Same Day Delivery Experience team to help ensure we have a compelling and friction-free ‘delivery in hours’ experience available to our customers, and help drive continuous innovation and improvement of the delivery experience across all phases of shopping, buying, and post purchase experience.
• Every single retail purchase placed on Amazon's retail websites uses our team’s software.
• Our systems process hundreds of millions of transactions per day.
• Our team’s work impacts millions of customers all over the world.

Key job responsibilities
• Build highly extensible software systems to meet the rapid growth of Amazon's delivery business and capabilities.
• Evolve and build services that are highly reliable and extremely low latency
• Design the direction of the checkout delivery experience working with multiple, different teams across the company
• Build the next generation systems for representing delivery choices to enable rapid experimentation for customers.

A day in the life
You will be working in a dynamic environment with a team of skilled software engineers to design and build technical solutions that meet business needs. The role involves ownership of designing new components that enhance the platform’s extensibility, scalability, availability, and latency. The team values delivering high-quality software and creating great customer experiences. We are looking for someone with experience in developing scalable, global software solutions.

About the team
We are the Same Day Delivery Experience team and are in the early innings of reinventing the Amazon shopping experience to make Amazon the first place customers think to shop when they need something today, wherever they are in the world.

jobs.title: Software Development Engineer II, WW Speed

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
- 7+ years of end to end product delivery experience
- Experience defining roadmap strategy and prioritizing deliverables for your team products
- Experience managing competing priorities and using metrics to drive business decisions
- Experience influencing multiple stakeholders and leading cross functional teams across geographies and business units
- 10+ years of Salesforce ecosystem experience
jobs.description:
Join Amazon Shipping (SWA) to build the next $100B initiative. Shape the future of shipping as a part of a new, emerging business and solve the biggest customer pain points in the small parcel logistics industry globally.

It’s no secret that Amazon relies on its technology to deliver millions of packages every day to its customers – on time, with low cost. We, the Product and Technology organization, simplify complex software solutions that work across our shippers, vendors, and warehouses to optimize both time & cost of getting the packages delivered. Our services already handle thousands of requests per second, make business decisions impacting billions of dollars a year, integrate with a network of small and large shippers, operators and drivers worldwide, manage business rules for millions of unique products, and improve the ordering and delivery experience for millions of online shoppers. That said, this remains a fast-growing business and our technical journey has only started. Our mission is to build the most efficient and optimal shipping solution on the planet, using our technology and engineering muscle as our biggest advantage. We aim to leverage top technologies in big data and optimization techniques to operate high volume, low latency, and high availability services.

What will you do?
As a Principal PM, you will be responsible for defining and owning the strategic vision and roadmap for Sales tooling products in the Amazon Shipping organization. This leader will work closely with stakeholders from Sales, Sales Ops and Marketing Ops to recommend and implement products to support the organization's strategic objectives around improving sales productivity and efficiency, customer experience, and cost optimization.

The ideal candidate has a strong background in Sales tooling products (Salesforce, Highspot, ZoomInfo, Marketo) combined with a solid understanding of current technologies (e.g. APIs, mobile, GenAI). S/he can develop and drive high-level strategic initiatives but also roll up their sleeves, dig in and get the job done. Maturity, strong business judgment, ability to influence cross-functional and remote teams, a customer-centric and creative mindset and strong analytical skills are essential. This opportunity requires excellent product management, prioritization, and communication skills, as well as the ability to navigate ambiguity.

Key job responsibilities
- Define the strategic vision and own the long-term roadmap for Sales tooling products across the Shipping organization
- Partner with Sales, Sales Ops and Marketing Ops to identify and influence development of new products and services that drive productivity and efficiency
- Work directly with engineering to build exceptional products by integrating 3P products into the broader ecosystem
- Define user stories and product requirements while contributing to the technical design of new product implementation and features
- Engage with stakeholders to prioritize features and drive go-to-market strategies while making appropriate trade-offs to optimize time-to-market, and technical and business outcomes
- Influence executive leadership and drive alignment across multiple teams on long-term priorities
- Display self-starter mentality and the ability to dive below the surface to uncover the "so what" rather than just the symptoms
- Up to 10% travel
jobs.description_short: Join Amazon Shipping (SWA) to build the next $100B initiative. Shape the future of shipping as a part of a new, emerging business and solve the biggest customer pain points in the small parcel logistics

jobs.title: Principal Product Manager, Ship with Amazon

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 6+ years of professional non-internship marketing experience
- Experience using data and metrics to drive improvements
- Experience with Excel or Tableau (data manipulation, macros, charts and pivot tables)
- Experience building, executing and scaling cross-functional marketing programs
- Experience leading go-to-market for consumer software or hardware product launches
jobs.description:
The Pick Up and Return Point (PARP) team manages a large network of Amazon lockers and counters worldwide where customers can pick up their orders or process returns. PARP exists to serve Amazon worldwide customers through a network of pickup and returns locations, in a way that improves transporter safety, reduces cost to serve, and enables incremental selection for Amazon, while making package handling effortless for customers, partners, and carriers. We accelerate adoption by driving customer awareness, conversion, retention, as well as partner satisfaction. Amazon Pickup and Returns (PARP) is experiencing tremendous growth and transformation. We are scaling rapidly from $322MM in net savings in 2025 toward our ambitious goal of $500MM in 2026 and $1B by 2028. This is an exciting time to join PARP as we build a world-class delivery experience for customers through alternate delivery options, including lockers and pickup points. If you are passionate about delivery operations and look around corners for ways to serve customers, we want your help.

The Senior Product Mkt Manager, Strategy will drive the strategic initiatives that enable PARP to achieve our growth targets. This role will own some of the largest and most visible projects across the organization, including our current focus on scaling returns through the PARP network. The individual will work closely with Country PARP teams, regional Ops teams, Retail and Logistics Service (RLS), Amazon Logistics (AMZL), science, technology, product, engineering, and central support teams to develop and execute strategies that accelerate the business flywheel.

The optimal fit for this role will be a candidate wanting to roll up his or her sleeves, dive into complex challenges, develop solutions and paths forward, and take senior management along the journey. This will be a high-visibility role requiring the candidate to drive critical business decisions in a fast-paced and often ambiguous environment. The role demands exceptional Earn Trust and Dive Deep capabilities, with responsibility for leading several high-stakes documents at the Beryl and L10 levels to influence senior leadership. A successful candidate will be customer-focused, highly analytical, resourceful, a big thinker, a strong writer with excellent document writing skills, and able to operate at all levels of the organization.

Key job responsibilities
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent
- 5+ years of end to end product delivery experience
- Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
- Experience in areas such as market research, statistics, finance or qualitative/quantitative research and proficient with SQL
- Experience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a technology product
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills
- Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, regions and driving GTM strategies
- Experience defining KPI's/SLA's used to drive multi-million dollar businesses and reporting to senior leadership

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

The Data Center Availability team works with the hundreds of AWS data centers around the globe to deliver the highest quality and lowest cost physical security, availability, capacity, and scaling results for our customers. Our aim is to standardize operations globally by delivering tools, policy, processes and procedures to our internal teams. The Availability tech team is seeking a System Engineer to design, develop, and implement software tools that streamline operations by automating tedious manual processes.

Key job responsibilities
As a System Engineer, you will:
- Design software tools using advanced automation methods such as Agentic AI
- Work with business stakeholders to understand the opportunities to automate the manual work and streamline the process
- Develop and maintain software pipelines using best practices for smooth production code release.
- Suggest and implement best practices for coding, design and operational excellence.
- Mentor engineers and develop them
jobs.description_short: AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers

jobs.description:
Amazon Worldwide Grocery Store Tech is seeking a talented Principal Product Manager-Tech (PMT) to help us grow and evolve our technology platform to further scale under the roof technology.

Amazon provides Prime members unparalleled convenience and ease of mind by offering one-hour, ultra fast delivery as well as two-hour scheduled delivery of tens and thousands of essential products.

To be successful you need to be flexible and entrepreneurial. You are a true owner - you are passionate about the business and innovate on behalf of our customers. You ideally have experience presenting a strategic vision to SVP/VP leadership, and are excited to think-out-of-the box to solve complex problems.

Key job responsibilities
- Define the long-term tech-product vision for our stores (e.g., Amazon Fresh stores, Whole Foods Market, Go stores).
- Define the metrics, and your work will have direct impact on the day-to-day operations.
- Own the product roadmap to deliver incrementally on the vision.
- Partner closely with engineering teams, UX teams, and operations stakeholders across NA, EU/UK, JP, SG, IN, and MENA.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 4+ years of building scripts, tooling, and automation for large-scale computing environments experience
- Bachelor's degree in Systems Engineering, Computer Science, or related field or relevant work experience
- Experience with service-oriented architecture and web services
- Experience maintaining the resilience and reliability of services by troubleshooting deployment failures, creating infrastructure as code, creating resources (alarms, metrics, IAM role credentials), infrastructure provisioning and patching hosts
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

The Data Center Availability team works with the hundreds of AWS data centers around the globe to deliver the highest quality and lowest cost physical security, availability, capacity, and scaling results for our customers. Our aim is to standardize operations globally by delivering tools, policy, processes and procedures to our internal teams. The Availability tech team is seeking a System Engineer to design, develop, and implement software tools that streamline operations by automating tedious manual processes.

Key job responsibilities
As a System Engineer, you will:
• Design software tools using advanced automation methods such as Agentic AI
* Work with business stakeholders to understand the opportunities to automate the manual work and streamline the process
* Develop and maintain software pipelines using best practices for smooth production code release.
* Suggest and implement best practices for coding, design and operational excellence.
* Mentor engineers and develop them
